title: The Wishing Ring

evidence.note: Published 1914; the 95-year US copyright term has expired for works published through 1930, so the film print is public domain in the US by term expiry.

diy_links.label: Stanford Copyright Renewal DB — search “The Wishing Ring” (renewals ~1941, 1942, 1943)

US Copyright Office public records (1978+ renewals/assignments)

editorial: The Wishing Ring: An Idyll of Old England is a 1914 American silent comedy-drama directed by Maurice Tourneur for the World Film Corporation and shot in Fort Lee, New Jersey, starring Vivian Martin. Though French-born Tourneur directed it, it is a US production; long thought lost, a print was later recovered and the film was added to the Library of Congress National Film Registry in 2012. As a 1914 US publication, the film print is public domain by expiry of the 95-year US copyright term; other layers remain undetermined.
